title: What I think about the Theory of Relativity

transcript.text: Hello, my name is Kyle Pounds, and this video is what I think about the theory of Relativity.
I can't remember right now the difference between the special theory and the general theory,
But the theory of Relativity was when Einstein,
I don't know how he figured it out, but he just contemplated and decided that maybe matter and energy were the same thing.
Maybe he was reading some Eastern texts or something.
So he figured out what that meant, and that was when he came up with, I think, what's it called, quantum physics,
Where he'd come up with the idea of the atom and just matter interacting with vibration.
Well, actually, I got sidetracked, sorry.
So he contemplated how time stops when you go to the speed of light.
And how that works is everything works at a vibration.
The reason people usually use it is the trains, but that's used in a macro world, and it just didn't make sense to me,
So I didn't understand it until I started thinking of it in the mini world,
Which is vibrations in your protons and neutrons, like, you know, the quarks or the strings vibrating.
Vibrate at a certain speed, and they can't vibrate any faster than that speed.
They vibrate at the speed of light.
And so, and apparently there's a mix, a fixed point in the universe,
So that if you move against that, you, you know, you're vibrating like this,
And you go, and then you start moving, when the little vibration has to move forward,
It can't go as fast compared to in the world of your cells
Than it could if it was just sitting still for yourself,
Because it has to go, it can only go the speed of light.
So, it's kind of like you're in a bus, and the bus is going through water at 20 miles an hour,
And there's no top on the bus.
And you can only, you have roller blades where the wheels only go forward,
And they lock if you go back, but if you push too far back, the lock slips, and you go back.
And that can only withstand 20 miles an hour from the wind that hits you.
So, you're standing on the bus, the bus goes 20 miles an hour, and that's all it can go,
And you're standing on it, and you go as fast as the bus.
If the bus isn't moving, you can ride those roller blades from the back to the front of the bus.
But if the bus is moving, you can't.
And it's kind of like that.
So, that's how time slows down wind, the faster you go.
And if you go, if you're going as fast as the speed of light, I guess time stops for you,
Because those little strings that are moving like this just freeze.
That has been tested in watches on airline pilots.
I think they'll have to regularly adjust their watches,
Because even though they're only going like 700 miles an hour,
They're going, it's incremental in how time actually slows down as you go faster.
So, did I do a good explanation, or is there something that I missed?
So, that's it.
Thank you.
